content.problemStatement:
Little Sajal went to school and learned how to calculate the score of an integer. The score of an integer is defined as the sum of the absolute value of the difference of all pairs of digits in this integer. For example, the score of 214 is ∣2−1∣+∣1−4∣+∣2−4∣=6|2 - 1| + |1 - 4| + |2 - 4| = 6∣2−1∣+∣1−4∣+∣2−4∣=6.

As Sajal is very smart, he quickly learned it, and as an exercise, the teacher gave him a task. He will be given two integers LLL and RRR, and he will be asked to calculate the sum of scores of all integers between LLL and RRR (inclusive).

As this task is a little bit hard for Sajal, he wants your help.


Input Format

The first number will be the number of test cases TTT (T≤104T \leq 10^4T≤104).
Then there will be TTT lines, each line containing two integers LLL and RRR (1≤L≤R≤10181 \leq L \leq R \leq 10^{18}1≤L≤R≤1018).


Output Format

For each test case, your program should output the desired sum in a single line. As the sum can be very large, output it modulo 1000000007.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T (1 <= T <= 10^4). Then, for each test case, there are two integers L and R (1 <= L <= R <= 10^18) on a single line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the sum of scores of all integers between L and R (inclusive) modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, the score of 214 is ∣2−1∣+∣1−4∣+∣2−4∣=6|2 - 1| + |1 - 4| + |2 - 4| = 6∣2−1∣+∣1−4∣+∣2−4∣=6.
For the second test case, the scores of 12, 13, 14, 15, and 16 are calculated and summed up to 15.
For the third test case, since there are no pairs of digits in single-digit numbers, the score is 0.
For the fourth test case, the scores of 257, 258, 259, and 260 are calculated and summed up to 48.
For the fifth test case, the score of 112 is ∣1−1∣+∣1−2∣+∣1−2∣=2|1 - 1| + |1 - 2| + |1 - 2| = 2∣1−1∣+∣1−2∣+∣1−2∣=2.
Explanation: The score of 100 is ∣1−0∣+∣0−0∣+∣1−0∣=2|1 - 0| + |0 - 0| + |1 - 0| = 2∣1−0∣+∣0−0∣+∣1−0∣=2. However, since there are no pairs of digits in single-digit numbers, the score is 0.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over all numbers from L to R and calculate the score for each number. The score is the sum of the absolute differences between all pairs of digits in the number. We can use a brute force approach to calculate the score for each number and then sum up all the scores. However, this approach would be inefficient for large inputs. A more efficient approach would be to use dynamic programming to calculate the sum of scores for all numbers up to a certain number, and then use this sum to calculate the sum of scores for the range [L, R].
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (R - L + 1) * log(R)) — explain: per query is O((R - L + 1) * log(R)), total is O(T * (R - L + 1) * log(R))
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — explain: we only use a constant amount of space to store the sum of scores and other variables
